codeReview.summary: The commit fixes one flake mode by introducing epsilon tolerance, but it weakens the monotonic invariant by allowing small backward cursor movement, which conflicts with the feature expectation that backward-advance bugs are not reintroduced.

issues.description: The new guard `newUpperBound.timeIntervalSince(currentBound) >= -monotonicEpsilon` accepts negative deltas up to 1ns, so a true backward move can pass. That violates the same file’s contract (`Any backward movement is strictly rejected regardless of magnitude`, line 96 in this commit snapshot) and the feature’s expected behavior to avoid reintroducing backward cursor movement bugs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`, but transcript evidence does not show the required `.factory/library/user-testing.md` read from step 1 of the assigned skill.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/indexing-efficiency-worker/SKILL.md` step 1 requires reading `mission.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.factory/library/architecture.md`, and `.factory/library/user-testing.md`; transcript skeleton for session `92dd6b3d-dd7f-4030-a222-20a232db5f21` shows reads of mission/validation and code files but no read of `.factory/library/user-testing.md`.

summary: Reviewed feature `fast-surfaces-stabilize-backfillscheduler-idempotent-test` at commit `33f9ac23ddb36df2d2bc9eff18c73a67f2e5d5c4`: transcript and diff were examined, and the feature is marked fail due to a blocking monotonicity regression risk (small backward movement now accepted).
